seoTitle: How to Show Labels on a Jira Board
metaDescription: Learn how to display issue labels directly on your Jira board's cards so you can quickly scan for context without opening each ticket.
intro: Labels can carry useful context, like which team or category an issue belongs to, but they're not always visible on the board by default. This guide covers turning on label display so you can scan for that context at a glance.

steps.heading: Open your board settings
steps.body: Go to the configuration menu for the specific board where you want labels to appear.

steps.heading: Find the card layout settings
steps.body: Locate the section that controls what additional fields display on each card.

steps.heading: Add labels as a visible field
steps.body: Select labels from the list of available fields to add them to the card display.

steps.heading: Save your changes
steps.body: Save the updated board configuration so the change takes effect.

steps.heading: Confirm labels appear on cards
steps.body: Return to the board view and check that labels now show up on issues that have them assigned.

faq.question: Why don't I see the option to add labels to the board?
faq.answer: This setting is usually found under the board's card layout or configuration options, so check there if it's not immediately visible.

faq.question: Will this show labels for every issue on the board?
faq.answer: Only issues that already have a label assigned will display one; issues without labels simply won't show that field.

faq.question: Can I show more than one field on a card besides labels?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira's card layout settings typically let you add multiple fields, not just labels, to customize what's visible at a glance.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to change board display settings?
faq.answer: Board configuration access can vary, but in many cases anyone with board admin rights can adjust the card layout.
